title: 42 vessels redirected from Iranian ports under blockade enforcement
summary: CENTCOM Commander Admiral Brad Cooper said US naval forces have now redirected 42 vessels attempting to violate the blockade of Iranian ports since April 13, an increase of three from the prior day, while 41 tankers carrying 69 million barrels of Iranian oil worth roughly $6 billion remain unable to reach buyers.
contents:
# 42 vessels redirected from Iranian ports under blockade enforcement

## Operational Tally
US Central Command confirmed that the naval blockade of Iranian ports, in effect since April 13, has now resulted in the redirection of 42 vessels that attempted to violate the cordon. Admiral Brad Cooper announced the figure on April 29, marking an increase of three vessels from the previous daily report. CENTCOM characterised enforcement as continuous and methodical, with ships warned and turned away rather than boarded in most cases.

## Cargo Stranded
Admiral Cooper described 41 tankers carrying 69 million barrels of Iranian crude oil as currently unable to reach buyers, with cargo value estimated at $6 billion. The cargo backlog reflects buyers cancelling lifts after blockade enforcement began and tankers either holding offshore or attempting alternate transshipments that US forces intercept.

## Force Posture Shift
The Washington Post reported on April 29 that the USS Gerald Ford carrier strike group will soon depart the Middle East, signalling a transition to a smaller but still robust regional posture. Admiral Cooper plans to brief President Trump on April 30 on additional military options against Iran, reportedly including a special operations forces raid to secure highly enriched uranium.
